messages.content: My basil keeps drooping by the afternoon even though I water it every morning. What am I doing wrong?

Drooping at the hottest part of the day can happen even when the soil is still moist. Check the soil an inch down before watering again, because daily watering can keep the roots too wet if the pot drains slowly.
So the problem could be overwatering, not underwatering?
Exactly. Basil likes steady moisture, but it also needs air around the roots. Let the potting mix guide you instead of the clock.
The internet works on my phone but not on my laptop. Should I reboot the router first?

Start smaller. Forget and reconnect to the Wi-Fi network on the laptop, then see whether the laptop gets a valid IP address. If the phone is online, the router may be fine and the laptop may just have stale network state.
Then reboot the laptop, and only after that restart the router. Changing one thing at a time makes it easier to see where the failure actually lives.
My sourdough starter doubles one day and barely moves the next. Is it dying?

Not necessarily. Starter activity swings with temperature, feeding ratio, and flour type. If it smells pleasantly sour and still shows bubbles, it is usually alive and just reacting to different conditions.
What change should I test first?
Keep the temperature steady for a few feedings. Consistent warmth often tells you more than changing flour, water, and timing all at once.
